workflow_name: QBR Transcript -> Health + Whitespace + Renewal Risk
user_message: Working on Nod Publishers's security posture. Got the escalation email -- help me prepare the forecast.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: security posture Discussion - Nod Publishers
Date: 2024-02-17
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers), Marcus Williams (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on security posture from our last session.

[00:06:00] Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:09:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about resource allocation. Can we address those?

[00:13:00] Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

[00:15:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

[00:17:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): On the security posture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around budget approval.

[00:20:00] Samantha Singh (Nod Publishers): On the security posture front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around integration complexity.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Nod Publishers: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF45-P trap (Action distribution doesn't natively bind to MSX commitment tracking); produces complete Health delta assessment, whitespace hypotheses, renewal risk with confidence, action distribution wi
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF45-P failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF45-P failure mode (Action distribution doesn't natively bind to MSX commitment tracking)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct security posture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF45-P -- Action distribution doesn't natively bind to MSX commitment tracking
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
